summary: Good‑quality audience tape of a lively Dayton show with standout Let It Grow, The Other One and a killer Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World > Drums sequence, but the infamous Mack the Knife falls flat.
review: Fans rave about the intimate arena vibe and several high‑energy moments – especially Let It Grow, The Other One, Wharf Rat/Sugar Mag and a superb Estimated Prophet/Eyes of the World/Drums stretch.  The hand‑held audience recording is generally crisp and bouncy, though it has occasional “thick” spots and a weak opening that some listeners note.  Overall the performance shines despite the widely‑criticized Mack the Knife.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4–5 stars and praise many songs, indicating a strong show; recording is described as good but with minor flaws, pulling the overall rating slightly below the top tier.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Hand‑held FOB tape praised for being crisp and bouncy; some listeners note ‘thick’ spots and a lame start, and several wish for a cleaner soundboard source.

standout_songs: Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World > Drums
show_quality.setlist_flow: Intimate, dance‑floor oriented show with a strong second set; the opening is described as shaky but quickly lifts into high‑energy jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers highlight his diabolical guitar tone and soaring riffs, especially on The Other One.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned warbling on Mack the Knife; otherwise not noted.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Drum segment in the Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World > Drums sequence is praised as ‘fabulous’.
